wal_level.title: Enable Logical Replication
wal_level.content: Supabase enables logical replication by default. This step is already complete.

replication_role.title: Grant Replication Permission
replication_role.content: Run the SQL below in the Supabase SQL Editor in your project dashboard.

wal2json.title: Verify wal2json
wal2json.content: wal2json is installed by default on Supabase.

replication_test.title: Test Replication Connection
replication_test.content: This step runs automatically when steps 1–3 are verified.

wal_level.title: Enable Logical Replication
wal_level.content:
1. Open your Neon project in the Neon console.
2. Navigate to Settings → Logical Replication.
3. Click 'Enable' to turn on logical replication.
4. Return here and click 'Verify'.

replication_role.title: Grant Replication Permission
replication_role.content: Run the SQL below in the Neon SQL Editor or via psql.

wal2json.title: Verify wal2json
wal2json.content: wal2json is available when logical replication is enabled on Neon.

replication_test.title: Test Replication Connection
replication_test.content: This step runs automatically when steps 1–3 are verified.

wal_level.title: Enable Logical Replication
wal_level.content:
1. Open the AWS RDS console.
2. Navigate to your DB Parameter Group.
3. Set rds.logical_replication to 1.
4. Apply the parameter group to your instance and reboot.
5. Return here and click 'Verify'.

replication_role.title: Grant Replication Permission
replication_role.content: Connect to your instance using the master user and run the SQL below.

wal2json.title: Verify wal2json
wal2json.content: wal2json is a supported plugin on AWS RDS, available without additional installation.

replication_test.title: Test Replication Connection
replication_test.content: This step runs automatically when steps 1–3 are verified.

wal_level.title: Enable Logical Replication
wal_level.content:
1. Open the AWS RDS console.
2. Navigate to your DB Parameter Group.
3. Set rds.logical_replication to 1.
4. Apply the parameter group to your instance and reboot.
5. Return here and click 'Verify'.

replication_role.title: Grant Replication Permission
replication_role.content: Connect to your instance using the master user and run the SQL below.

wal2json.title: Verify wal2json
wal2json.content: wal2json is supported on Aurora PostgreSQL.

replication_test.title: Test Replication Connection
replication_test.content: This step runs automatically when steps 1–3 are verified.

wal_level.title: Enable Logical Decoding
wal_level.content:
1. Open the Google Cloud Console.
2. Navigate to your Cloud SQL instance.
3. Under Edit → Database flags, set cloudsql.logical_decoding to on.
4. Save and restart the instance.
5. Return here and click 'Verify'.

replication_role.title: Grant Replication Permission
replication_role.content: Connect to your Cloud SQL instance and run the SQL below.

wal2json.title: Verify wal2json
wal2json.content: wal2json is supported natively on Google Cloud SQL.

replication_test.title: Test Replication Connection
replication_test.content: This step runs automatically when steps 1–3 are verified.

wal_level.title: Enable Logical Replication
wal_level.content:
1. Open the Azure portal.
2. Navigate to your Azure Database for PostgreSQL server.
3. Under Server parameters, set azure.replication_support to logical.
4. Save and restart the server.
5. Return here and click 'Verify'.

replication_role.title: Grant Replication Permission
replication_role.content: Connect to your Azure PostgreSQL server and run the SQL below.

wal2json.title: Verify wal2json
wal2json.content: wal2json is supported as a logical decoding plugin on Azure.

replication_test.title: Test Replication Connection
replication_test.content: This step runs automatically when steps 1–3 are verified.

wal_level.title: Enable Logical Replication
wal_level.content:
1. Open your postgresql.conf file (usually at /etc/postgresql/VERSION/main/postgresql.conf).
2. Set wal_level = logical.
3. Also set max_replication_slots = 5 and max_wal_senders = 5 (or higher if needed).
4. Restart the PostgreSQL service with sudo systemctl restart postgresql.
5. Return here and click 'Verify'.

replication_role.title: Grant Replication Permission
replication_role.content: Connect as the postgres superuser and run the SQL below.

wal2json.title: Verify wal2json
wal2json.content: wal2json must be installed on your PostgreSQL server. See https://github.com/eulerto/wal2json for installation. Add wal2json to shared_preload_libraries in postgresql.conf and restart.

replication_test.title: Test Replication Connection
replication_test.content: This step runs automatically when steps 1–3 are verified.

wal_level.title: Enable Logical Replication
wal_level.content:
1. Edit postgresql.conf or use ALTER SYSTEM SET wal_level = 'logical'.
2. Set max_replication_slots and max_wal_senders as needed.
3. Restart PostgreSQL.
4. Return here and click 'Verify'.

replication_role.title: Grant Replication Permission
replication_role.content: Connect as a superuser and run the SQL below.

wal2json.title: Verify wal2json
wal2json.content: Ensure wal2json is installed. See https://github.com/eulerto/wal2json. Add to shared_preload_libraries if self-hosted.

replication_test.title: Test Replication Connection
replication_test.content: This step runs automatically when steps 1–3 are verified.
